title: Add HorizontalPodAutoscaler sub-reconciler
description:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** high
**Source:** HorizontalPodAutoscaler Sub-Reconciler

Add an optional `spec.autoscaling` field to the Keystone CRD that, when set, creates a `HorizontalPodAutoscaler` targeting the Keystone API Deployment. The HPA is reconciled via a new `reconcileHPA` sub-reconciler following the established condition contract pattern (`HPAReady` added to `subConditionTypes`). When `spec.autoscaling` is removed, the HPA is explicitly deleted. This enables production workloads to auto-scale based on CPU and/or memory utilization.

The sub-reconciler implements the optional sub-resource lifecycle with three paths:
1. **spec.autoscaling nil + no existing HPA** → set `HPAReady=True` reason `HPANotRequired`, return early
2. **spec.autoscaling nil + existing HPA found** → explicit `client.Delete`, set `HPAReady=True` reason `HPANotRequired`
3. **spec.autoscaling set** → call `EnsureHPA` (create-or-update with `SetControllerReference`), set `HPAReady=True` reason `HPAReady`

Includes `AutoscalingSpec` struct (`MinReplicas`, `MaxReplicas`, `TargetCPUUtilization`, `TargetMemoryUtilization`), kubebuilder validation markers, CEL `XValidation` requiring at least one metric target, RBAC markers for `autoscaling` group, `Owns(&autoscalingv2.HorizontalPodAutoscaler{})` watch, and unit tests.

**Affected Areas:**
- operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa_test.go
- operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go
- operators/keystone/internal/common/deployment/deployment.go

stories.title: Operator user enables CPU-based autoscaling for Keystone API
stories.want: to set spec.autoscaling with a targetCPUUtilization so that the Keystone API Deployment is autoscaled based on CPU usage
stories.so_that: production workloads handle variable load without manual replica management
criteria: Setting spec.autoscaling with targetCPUUtilization creates an HPA targeting the {name}-api Deployment
HPA minReplicas and maxReplicas match the values from spec.autoscaling
HPA metrics list contains a CPU resource metric with the specified average utilization target
HPAReady condition is set to True with reason HPAReady after successful reconciliation
HPA has an ownerReference pointing to the Keystone CR

stories.title: Operator user enables memory-based autoscaling for Keystone API
stories.want: to set spec.autoscaling with a targetMemoryUtilization so that the Keystone API Deployment is autoscaled based on memory usage
stories.so_that: memory-intensive workloads scale appropriately without CPU being the only signal
criteria: Setting spec.autoscaling with targetMemoryUtilization creates an HPA with a memory resource metric
HPA metrics list contains a memory resource metric with the specified average utilization target
Both CPU and memory metrics can be specified simultaneously
HPAReady condition is set to True with reason HPAReady

stories.title: Operator user removes autoscaling from Keystone CR
stories.want: to remove spec.autoscaling so that the HPA is deleted and Keystone reverts to static replica management
stories.so_that: I can cleanly transition between autoscaled and fixed-replica operation
criteria: Removing spec.autoscaling (setting to nil) deletes the existing HPA
HPAReady condition is set to True with reason HPANotRequired
No HPA resource remains in the namespace for this Keystone instance
Deployment continues to run with spec.replicas as its replica count

stories.title: Operator user creates Keystone CR without autoscaling
stories.want: the Keystone operator to skip HPA creation when spec.autoscaling is not set
stories.so_that: the default behavior remains unchanged and the aggregate Ready condition is not blocked
criteria: When spec.autoscaling is nil, no HPA is created
HPAReady condition is set to True with reason HPANotRequired
The aggregate Ready condition includes HPAReady and is not blocked by it
No HPA exists for the Keystone instance

stories.title: Operator user submits invalid autoscaling spec
stories.want: to receive clear validation errors when my autoscaling configuration is invalid
stories.so_that: I can fix my configuration before it reaches the controller
criteria: CEL XValidation rejects spec.autoscaling when neither targetCPUUtilization nor targetMemoryUtilization is set
Kubebuilder validation rejects maxReplicas < 1
Kubebuilder validation rejects targetCPUUtilization outside 1-100 range
Kubebuilder validation rejects targetMemoryUtilization outside 1-100 range

requirements.description: The operator SHALL create a HorizontalPodAutoscaler targeting the Keystone API Deployment when spec.autoscaling is set
requirements.rationale: Core functionality: the HPA enables CPU/memory-based autoscaling of the Keystone API pods

scenarios.name: HPA created with CPU metric
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling is set with targetCPUUtilization=80 and maxReplicas=10
scenarios.then: an HPA is created with scaleTargetRef pointing to {name}-api Deployment, maxReplicas=10, and a CPU resource metric at 80% average utilization
and_then: HPAReady condition is set to True with reason HPAReady

scenarios.name: HPA created with memory metric
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling is set with targetMemoryUtilization=70 and maxReplicas=5
scenarios.then: an HPA is created with a memory resource metric at 70% average utilization
and_then: HPAReady condition is set to True with reason HPAReady

scenarios.name: HPA created with both metrics
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling is set with both targetCPUUtilization=80 and targetMemoryUtilization=70
scenarios.then: the HPA metrics list contains both CPU and memory resource metrics
and_then: HPAReady condition is set to True with reason HPAReady

requirements.description: The operator SHALL set an ownerReference on the HPA so it is garbage-collected when the Keystone CR is deleted
requirements.rationale: Prevents orphaned HPAs when the Keystone CR is deleted; follows the established SetControllerReference pattern used by EnsureDeployment, EnsureService, and EnsurePDB

scenarios.name: HPA has ownerReference on creation
scenarios.when: an HPA is created by EnsureHPA
scenarios.then: the HPA has a controller ownerReference pointing to the Keystone CR
and_then: ownerReference.name equals the Keystone CR name

scenarios.name: HPA ownerReference restored after out-of-band removal
scenarios.when: the HPA exists but its ownerReference has been removed out-of-band
scenarios.then: EnsureHPA re-applies the ownerReference on the next reconciliation
and_then: the HPA is updated with the correct ownerReference

requirements.description: The operator SHALL explicitly delete the HPA when spec.autoscaling is removed from the Keystone CR
requirements.rationale: Owner reference handles CR deletion, but explicit deletion is needed when the field is removed to avoid leaving a stale HPA that conflicts with manual replica management

scenarios.name: HPA deleted when spec.autoscaling removed
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling was set (HPA exists) and is then removed (set to nil)
scenarios.then: the existing HPA is explicitly deleted via client.Delete
and_then: HPAReady condition is set to True with reason HPANotRequired

scenarios.name: No error when HPA already absent
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling is nil and no HPA exists for this instance
scenarios.then: reconcileHPA sets HPAReady=True with reason HPANotRequired and returns without error

requirements.description: The operator SHALL set the HPAReady condition following the sub-reconciler condition contract with ObservedGeneration
requirements.rationale: HPAReady must participate in the aggregate Ready condition via conditions.AllTrue; ObservedGeneration is required by the established condition contract pattern

scenarios.name: HPAReady True when autoscaling is active
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling is set and EnsureHPA succeeds
scenarios.then: HPAReady condition is True with reason HPAReady and ObservedGeneration equals keystone.Generation

scenarios.name: HPAReady True when autoscaling is not required
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling is nil
scenarios.then: HPAReady condition is True with reason HPANotRequired and ObservedGeneration equals keystone.Generation
and_then: message indicates autoscaling is not configured

scenarios.name: HPAReady participates in aggregate Ready
scenarios.when: HPAReady is added to subConditionTypes
scenarios.then: the aggregate Ready condition requires HPAReady=True to be True
and_then: conditions.AllTrue includes HPAReady in its check

requirements.description: The CRD SHALL validate the AutoscalingSpec with kubebuilder markers and CEL XValidation
requirements.rationale: Prevents invalid configurations from reaching the controller; CEL XValidation enforces that at least one metric target is specified, consistent with existing XValidation on DatabaseSpec and CacheSpec

scenarios.name: At least one metric target required
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling is set with neither targetCPUUtilization nor targetMemoryUtilization
scenarios.then: the API server rejects the CR with a validation error from the CEL rule
and_then: error message indicates at least one of targetCPUUtilization or targetMemoryUtilization must be set

scenarios.name: MaxReplicas minimum enforced
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling.maxReplicas is set to 0
scenarios.then: the API server rejects the CR with a validation error

scenarios.name: Target utilization range enforced
scenarios.when: spec.autoscaling.targetCPUUtilization is set to 0 or 101
scenarios.then: the API server rejects the CR with a validation error
and_then: error indicates value must be between 1 and 100

requirements.description: The EnsureHPA common helper SHALL follow the get-or-create/update pattern with metadata reconciliation
requirements.rationale: Consistent with EnsurePDB pattern: create with SetControllerReference, update with ownerRef/labels/annotations reconciliation, skip API write when nothing changed

scenarios.name: HPA created when not found
scenarios.when: no HPA exists with the expected name
scenarios.then: EnsureHPA creates the HPA with SetControllerReference and returns nil
and_then: the HPA has the correct spec and ownerReference

scenarios.name: HPA updated when spec changes
scenarios.when: an HPA exists but its spec differs from the desired state
scenarios.then: EnsureHPA updates the spec, labels, annotations, and ownerReference
and_then: the API write only occurs if something actually changed

scenarios.name: No API write when nothing changed
scenarios.when: EnsureHPA is called with the same desired state as the existing HPA
scenarios.then: no Update call is made to the API server

requirements.description: The controller SHALL have RBAC permissions for the autoscaling API group and SHALL watch owned HPAs
requirements.rationale: Without RBAC markers, the controller cannot manage HPAs; without the Owns watch, HPA changes are not reconciled

scenarios.name: RBAC marker present
scenarios.when: the controller is deployed
scenarios.then: the generated ClusterRole includes get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete on autoscaling/horizontalpodautoscalers

scenarios.name: Owns watch registered
scenarios.when: SetupWithManager is called
scenarios.then: the controller watches HorizontalPodAutoscaler resources via Owns()
and_then: changes to owned HPAs trigger reconciliation

requirements.description: The reconcileHPA sub-reconciler SHALL propagate errors from EnsureHPA and HPA deletion
requirements.rationale: Error propagation ensures the reconciler retries on transient failures and the operator surfaces problems via status conditions

scenarios.name: EnsureHPA error propagated
scenarios.when: EnsureHPA returns an error (e.g., API server unreachable)
scenarios.then: reconcileHPA wraps and returns the error

scenarios.name: Delete error propagated
scenarios.when: client.Delete returns an error during HPA cleanup
scenarios.then: reconcileHPA wraps and returns the error

tasks.title: Add AutoscalingSpec struct and Autoscaling field to KeystoneSpec with kubebuilder markers and CEL XValidation (REQ-005)
tasks.description: In `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go`: (1) Add `AutoscalingSpec` struct with fields `MinReplicas *int32` (optional, kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1), `MaxReplicas int32` (required, kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1), `TargetCPUUtilization *int32` (optional, kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1,Maximum=100), `TargetMemoryUtilization *int32` (optional, kubebuilder:validation:Minimum=1,Maximum=100). (2) Add CEL XValidation rule on AutoscalingSpec: `has(self.targetCPUUtilization) || has(self.targetMemoryUtilization)` with message 'at least one of targetCPUUtilization or targetMemoryUtilization must be set'. (3) Add `Autoscaling *AutoscalingSpec` field to `KeystoneSpec` with `+optional` and `json:"autoscaling,omitempty"` tags, following the existing `Federation *FederationSpec` pointer-for-optional pattern. (4) Add unit test for zero-value check in `keystone_types_test.go` verifying `spec.Autoscaling` is nil by default. (5) Run `make generate` to regenerate deepcopy.

tasks.title: Add EnsureHPA common helper function to internal/common/deployment/deployment.go with unit tests (REQ-006)
tasks.description: In `internal/common/deployment/deployment.go`: (1) Add `autoscalingv2 "k8s.io/api/autoscaling/v2"` import. (2) Implement `EnsureHPA(ctx, c, scheme, owner, hpa)` following EnsurePDB pattern exactly: Get→IsNotFound→Create with SetControllerReference; on existing: snapshot via DeepCopy, re-apply SetControllerReference, merge labels, merge annotations, set spec, guard Update with apiequality.Semantic.DeepEqual check on Spec/Labels/Annotations/OwnerReferences. Returns `error` only. (3) Add `autoscalingv2` scheme registration to `newScheme()` in `deployment_test.go`. (4) Add `testHPA()` helper returning a minimal HPA. (5) Add tests: `TestEnsureHPA_creates`, `TestEnsureHPA_updatesExisting`, `TestEnsureHPA_idempotent`, `TestEnsureHPA_reconciles_ownerRef_on_update`, `TestEnsureHPA_merges_labels_on_update`, `TestEnsureHPA_merges_annotations_on_update` — all mirroring the EnsurePDB test structure.

tasks.title: Add reconcileHPA sub-reconciler with buildKeystoneHPA and three-path lifecycle logic (REQ-001, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create new file `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa.go`: (1) Implement `reconcileHPA(ctx, keystone, configMapName) (ctrl.Result, error)` with three paths: Path 1 — spec.autoscaling nil + no existing HPA (IsNotFound) → set HPAReady=True reason HPANotRequired, return early. Path 2 — spec.autoscaling nil + existing HPA found → client.Delete, set HPAReady=True reason HPANotRequired. Path 3 — spec.autoscaling set → call buildKeystoneHPA, call deployment.EnsureHPA, set HPAReady=True reason HPAReady. Error wrapping: 'ensuring HorizontalPodAutoscaler' and 'deleting HorizontalPodAutoscaler'. HPA name: `{keystone.Name}-api` (same as Deployment). (2) Implement `buildKeystoneHPA(keystone)` returning `*autoscalingv2.HorizontalPodAutoscaler` with: scaleTargetRef={apiVersion:apps/v1, kind:Deployment, name:{name}-api}, minReplicas from spec.autoscaling.MinReplicas, maxReplicas from spec.autoscaling.MaxReplicas, metrics built conditionally from TargetCPUUtilization and TargetMemoryUtilization (only non-nil fields), commonLabels. (3) All conditions use conditions.SetCondition with ObservedGeneration=keystone.Generation.

tasks.title: Add reconcileHPA unit tests covering all three lifecycle paths and error scenarios (REQ-001, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create new file `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_hpa_test.go`: (1) `hpaTestScheme()` registering clientgoscheme, autoscalingv2, keystonev1alpha1. (2) `hpaTestKeystone()` returning minimal valid CR (reuse pattern from deployTestKeystone). (3) `newHPATestReconciler(s, objs...)` with fake client, status subresource, fake recorder. (4) Tests: `TestReconcileHPA_AutoscalingNil_NoExistingHPA_SetsHPANotRequired` — verifies HPAReady=True reason=HPANotRequired, no HPA created. `TestReconcileHPA_AutoscalingNil_ExistingHPA_DeletesHPA` — pre-creates HPA, verifies deletion and HPANotRequired condition. `TestReconcileHPA_AutoscalingSet_CreatesHPA` — verifies HPA created with correct scaleTargetRef, metrics, ownerRef. `TestReconcileHPA_AutoscalingSet_CPUOnly` — CPU metric only, no memory metric. `TestReconcileHPA_AutoscalingSet_MemoryOnly` — memory metric only, no CPU metric. `TestReconcileHPA_AutoscalingSet_BothMetrics` — both CPU and memory metrics present. `TestReconcileHPA_AutoscalingSet_MinReplicasOmitted` — MinReplicas nil on HPA. `TestReconcileHPA_AutoscalingSet_MinReplicasSet` — MinReplicas correctly propagated. `TestReconcileHPA_ConditionObservedGeneration` — ObservedGeneration matches keystone.Generation for both active and not-required paths. `TestReconcileHPA_EnsureError_Propagated` — interceptor-injected create error, verifies error wrapping. `TestReconcileHPA_DeleteError_Propagated` — interceptor-injected delete error, verifies error wrapping. `TestReconcileHPA_HPALabelsAndOwnerReference` — verifies commonLabels and ownerReference on created HPA. `TestBuildKeystoneHPA_ScaleTargetRef` — verifies scaleTargetRef points to correct Deployment. `TestBuildKeystoneHPA_Labels` — verifies commonLabels on HPA ObjectMeta.

tasks.title: Wire reconcileHPA into main controller: subConditionTypes, RBAC, Owns watch, reconcile chain (REQ-004, REQ-007)
tasks.description: In `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`: (1) Add `autoscalingv2 "k8s.io/api/autoscaling/v2"` import. (2) Add `"HPAReady"` to `subConditionTypes` slice (after DeploymentReady, before BootstrapReady). (3) Add RBAC marker: `// +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=autoscaling,resources=horizontalpodautoscalers,verbs=get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete` (after existing policy RBAC marker). (4) Add `Owns(&autoscalingv2.HorizontalPodAutoscaler{})` to SetupWithManager (after existing Owns(&policyv1.PodDisruptionBudget{})). (5) Add reconcileHPA call after reconcileDeployment in the Reconcile method: `if result, err := r.reconcileHPA(ctx, &keystone, configMapName); !result.IsZero() || err != nil { return r.updateStatus(ctx, &keystone, result, err) }` — between the reconcileDeployment and reconcileBootstrap blocks.

tasks.title: Add reference documentation for the spec.autoscaling field
tasks.description: Create `docs/reference/backend/keystone-autoscaling.md`: (1) Document the AutoscalingSpec struct fields with types, constraints, and defaults. (2) Document the CEL validation rule. (3) Document the three reconciliation paths (autoscaling nil/existing HPA/active). (4) Include a minimal example YAML snippet showing spec.autoscaling with CPU and memory targets. (5) Reference the HPAReady condition semantics (HPAReady vs HPANotRequired reasons).

review_criteria: AutoscalingSpec has correct kubebuilder validation markers (Minimum/Maximum) and CEL XValidation rule requiring at least one metric target
EnsureHPA follows EnsurePDB pattern exactly: create with SetControllerReference, update with DeepCopy+apiequality guard, label/annotation merge, ownerRef reconciliation
reconcileHPA correctly implements all three lifecycle paths: nil+no-HPA (skip), nil+existing-HPA (delete), set (ensure)
HPAReady condition always includes ObservedGeneration=keystone.Generation and uses correct reasons (HPAReady vs HPANotRequired)
RBAC marker includes all required verbs (get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete) for autoscaling group
All unit tests pass: EnsureHPA tests in deployment_test.go, reconcileHPA tests in reconcile_hpa_test.go, types test in keystone_types_test.go
Error messages from reconcileHPA wrap errors with descriptive context ('ensuring HorizontalPodAutoscaler', 'deleting HorizontalPodAutoscaler')
buildKeystoneHPA produces correct scaleTargetRef (apiVersion, kind, name) and conditional metrics list
go vet and golangci-lint pass with no errors on all modified packages
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────────┐
│         Keystone CR                │
│  spec.autoscaling: *AutoscalingSpec│
└────────────┬───────────────────────┘
             │
             ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────┐
│       reconcileHPA()               │
│  ├─ autoscaling nil?               │
│  │  ├─ HPA exists? → Delete        │
│  │  └─ no HPA → skip               │
│  └─ autoscaling set?               │
│     └─ buildKeystoneHPA()          │
│        └─ EnsureHPA()              │
└────────────┬───────────────────────┘
             │
             ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────┐
│   HPA (autoscaling/v2)             │
│   scaleTargetRef: {name}-api       │
│   metrics: [cpu, memory]           │
└────────────┬───────────────────────┘
             │
             ▼
┌────────────────────────────────────┐
│   Deployment: {name}-api           │
└────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Data Flow

1. User sets `spec.autoscaling` on Keystone CR
2. Controller reconcile loop reaches `reconcileHPA` (after `reconcileDeployment`)
3. `reconcileHPA` reads `spec.autoscaling` pointer
4. If set: builds HPA via `buildKeystoneHPA`, calls `EnsureHPA` (create-or-update)
5. If nil: checks for existing HPA, deletes if found
6. Sets `HPAReady` condition in both paths
7. HPA targets `{name}-api` Deployment via `scaleTargetRef`

## Key Decisions

- **Separate file for reconcileHPA**: Unlike PDB which is embedded in reconcileDeployment, HPA has its own condition type and optional lifecycle, warranting its own file.
- **EnsureHPA follows EnsurePDB exactly**: Same get-or-create/update pattern with metadata reconciliation and idempotency guard.
- **HPA name matches Deployment name**: `{name}-api` — consistent naming, easy to correlate.
- **spec.replicas unchanged**: The Deployment keeps spec.replicas as initial/fallback; HPA governs actual scaling range via minReplicas/maxReplicas.
- **configMapName parameter**: Passed for chain consistency even though reconcileHPA doesn't use it — all sub-reconcilers share the same signature.

## Pitfalls

- Must run `make generate` after types change to regenerate deepcopy methods.
- Adding HPAReady to subConditionTypes causes a brief Ready=False transition for existing CRs until the next reconciliation sets HPAReady=True.
- The HPA scaleTargetRef.name MUST match `fmt.Sprintf("%s-api", keystone.Name)` exactly — verify in buildKeystoneHPA tests.
